story: Each April, Congress ritually “reaffirmed” physics; this year, the calm snapped. Newtonians and Einsteinians argued under a solemnly swinging glass pendulum while crowds shouted outside; Representative Mallory warned that ignoring gravity would send oceans and children into the sky. That morning, in a basement lab at the National Institute of Standards, Dr. Liao’s atomic clocks and interferometer showed that past roll calls had never budged a constant. Her memo—“Nature won’t notice today”—sat embargoed on a server. By late afternoon, coffee cups ringed desks. The Speaker called two roll calls—Relativity failed, then Newton failed. Papers shuffled. “Both motions have failed. The House is adjourned,” he said.
implausible_knowledge: That morning, in a basement lab at the National Institute of Standards, Dr. Liao’s atomic clocks and interferometer showed that past roll calls had never budged a constant. Her memo—“Nature won’t notice today”—sat embargoed on a server.
question: Right after the Speaker adjourns and tension spikes on the House floor, what will Representative Mallory most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Stay calm and reassure colleagues that nothing will change, encouraging an orderly, unhurried exit.
answer_without_knowledge: Brace herself and urge colleagues to hold on, fearing a sudden change in reality.
